problem: The application needs a changeable list of favorite restaurants. Currently, no restaurant or favorites concept exists in the codebase. A new data model, API routes, UI components, and i18n strings are required. The existing counter feature provides a complete reference pattern (schema, API route, validation, form component, server component, i18n, tests).

files.why: Drizzle ORM schema file where a new favorite-restaurants table must be defined. Currently contains only counterSchema (lines 16-24). Referenced by drizzle.config.ts as the single schema source.
Drizzle Kit config pointing to src/models/Schema.ts for migrations. After schema change, `npm run db:generate` produces a new migration.
Migration output directory. Currently has one migration (0000_init-db.sql for counter table). A new migration file will be generated here.
Drizzle migration journal tracking applied migrations. New migration entry will be appended.
Creates Drizzle DB connection with `import * as schema from '@/models/Schema'`. New schema exports are automatically available via the wildcard import.
Singleton DB instance used by API routes and server components. No changes needed but is the import point for data access.
Reference pattern for API route: Zod validation, Drizzle insert/upsert, NextResponse JSON. New API routes for favorite restaurants should follow this pattern.
Reference pattern for Zod validation schema. A new validation file for restaurant data will follow this structure.
Reference pattern for client-side form: 'use client', react-hook-form with zodResolver, fetch to API, router.refresh(). New restaurant list UI should follow this pattern.
Reference pattern for server component with DB read: uses db.query, getTranslations, headers. New restaurant list display should follow this pattern.
Dashboard page behind auth. A favorites feature is user-specific, so it likely belongs in the dashboard (auth) route group.
Dashboard layout with navigation links. A new favorites page would need a nav entry added here.
Auth layout wrapping ClerkProvider. All pages in this route group are behind Clerk auth, providing user identity.
English i18n strings. New namespace and keys needed for favorite restaurants feature.
French i18n strings. Must stay in sync with en.json for new feature keys.
Environment variable validation with @t3-oss/env-nextjs. DATABASE_URL (line 8) and CLERK_SECRET_KEY (line 7) are already validated. No new env vars expected.
App configuration including locale list and prefix strategy. No changes expected but governs i18n behavior.
Exports Link, usePathname, useRouter from next-intl navigation. Used by client components for locale-aware navigation.
Shared layout template with leftNav/rightNav slots. Dashboard layout uses this for navigation rendering.
Scripts and dependencies. Key commands: build-local, lint, check:types, check:deps, check:i18n, test, test:e2e, db:generate, db:migrate.
Pre-commit hooks: ultracite lint/fix and knip dependency check. Commits must pass these gates.
Reference pattern for integration tests using Playwright: PUT requests, status assertions, JSON response validation.
Coding standards: named exports only, Zod type-only imports, no useMemo/useCallback, Tailwind v4 utility classes, conventional commits, test naming conventions.
Server component using currentUser() from @clerk/nextjs/server to access authenticated user. Shows how to get user identity for user-scoped data.

facts: No restaurant, favorite, or list concept exists anywhere in the codebase.
The only existing DB table is 'counter' with columns: id (serial PK), count (integer), updated_at, created_at.
Drizzle ORM (v0.45.1) with PostgreSQL dialect is the database layer; schema defined in src/models/Schema.ts.
Database migrations are generated via `npm run db:generate` (drizzle-kit generate) and applied via `npm run db:migrate`.
Migration journal (migrations/meta/_journal.json) currently has one entry at index 0.
Authentication is handled by Clerk (v7.0.6). The (auth) route group wraps pages in ClerkProvider.
User identity is accessible server-side via `currentUser()` from '@clerk/nextjs/server' (seen in Hello.tsx line 1).
No middleware.ts exists in the project. Auth protection relies on Clerk's ClerkProvider and route group structure.
i18n uses next-intl with two locales: 'en' and 'fr'. All user-visible strings must be in both locale files.
Forms use react-hook-form (v7.71.2) with @hookform/resolvers and Zod (v4.3.6) for validation.
API routes return NextResponse.json(); validation errors return status 422.
The counter API route pattern uses an upsert (insert + onConflictDoUpdate) and .returning().
Server components read data directly via db.query (e.g., CurrentCount.tsx).
Client components use 'use client' directive and fetch() to API routes.
AGENTS.md mandates: named exports, absolute imports via @/, no default exports except Next.js pages, Tailwind v4 utility classes, conventional commits.
Quality gates: lint (ultracite check --type-aware --type-check), check:types (tsc --noEmit), check:deps (knip), check:i18n (i18n-check).
Testing stack: Vitest for unit tests (*.test.ts), Playwright for integration (*.spec.ts) and e2e (*.e2e.ts).
Pre-commit hooks via lefthook: commitlint for commit messages, ultracite for lint/format, knip for unused dependency detection.
The counter feature is in the (marketing) route group (public). A user-specific favorites feature should be in the (auth) route group (protected).
DBConnection.ts uses `import * as schema` wildcard, so new schema exports are automatically picked up.
Next.js version is 16.2.1 with React 19.2.4 and React Compiler enabled in production.
unknowns: Whether 'changeable list' implies full CRUD (create, read, update, delete) or a simpler add/remove toggle pattern.
Whether the favorite restaurants list should be user-scoped (per authenticated user) or global. User-scoping is strongly implied by 'my favorite' but not confirmed.
What data fields a restaurant entry should have (name only, or also address, cuisine type, rating, notes, etc.).
Whether restaurants should be a fixed catalog the user selects from, or free-form entries the user creates.
Whether list ordering/reordering is part of the 'changeable' requirement.
Whether the feature needs its own dedicated page/route or should be embedded in the existing dashboard page.
How Clerk user IDs should be stored in the DB (the current schema has no user-scoping pattern to reference).
